generated_oml:
instance BrightnessPhotometricSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Photometric sensor measuring brightness in Living Lab office space"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BrightnessDataTransmitted
]

instance ColorTemperaturePhotometricSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Photometric sensor measuring color temperature in Kelvin for Living Lab office space"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ColorTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance BrightnessDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BrightnessPhotometricSensor
]

instance ColorTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ColorTemperaturePhotometricSensor
]

instance BrightnessData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time brightness measurements from photometric sensors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BrightnessDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, EclipseHono
]

instance ColorTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time color temperature measurements in Kelvin from photometric sensors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ColorTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, EclipseHono
]

instance LightActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting system actuator managed via LightTwinComponent and DittoController"
]

instance RevitIFCModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM-based geometric representation using Revit IFC format enriched with device metadata for sensors and actuators"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDE
]

instance LightTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured DT artifact for lighting system components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDE
]

instance LightFeatureParser : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "FeatureParser controller for lighting properties ('on', 'brightness', color temp kelvin')"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDE
]

instance MDE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering (DSR-guided) for automating BIM-to-DT artifact generation (R2) and modular architecture extension points (R4) for service-oriented custom analytics"
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseBasyx : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source Industry 4.0 platform for data integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance BentleyItwinJS : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bentley Systems' itwin.js library for infrastructure digital twin application development"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance TwinLightFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TwinLight research framework for digital twin development"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, CustomAnalyticsService
]

instance BOREALISFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BOREALIS research framework for digital twin development"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, CustomAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Policy-based validation for sensor data (value ranges, units)"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DirectControlService
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "At-least-once message delivery for sensor data transmission"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DirectControlService
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization for Facility Managers to monitor system states"
    DTDFVocab:provides MonitorSystemStateInsight
]

instance DirectControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Direct control of physical infrastructure through the twin interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPhysicalInfrastructureAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operational efficiency optimization through predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveInsight
]

instance CustomAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Custom analytics services integrated via modular architecture extension points (R4)"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomInsight
]

instance MonitorSystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time system state monitoring for Facility Managers"
]

instance PredictiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive insights for operational efficiency optimization"
]

instance CustomInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Custom analytics insights from modular extension points"
]

instance ControlPhysicalInfrastructureAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote control of physical infrastructure via digital twin interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LivingLabOfficeEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Building within AECO domain for Living Lab office space with BCS components"
    base:contains BrightnessPhotometricSensor, ColorTemperaturePhotometricSensor, LightActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals are transmitted to Building Control Systems (BCS) using Eclipse Hono and Ditto for bidirectional communication, enabling Facility Managers and Building Control Engineers to adjust physical infrastructure via the digital twin interface."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Centralized hub architecture with coordinated roles (Building Control Engineer, Facility Manager, DT Engineer) facilitating bidirectional data exchange and modular integration of services for holistic building life cycle management."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle comprises design-time modeling within BIM where Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers to establish semantic foundations; automated deployment via model-driven engineering (MDE) generating deployable artifacts with minimal manual intervention (R2); runtime operation featuring real-time data synchronization using Eclipse Hono and Ditto (R3) for physical-digital alignment; and evolution through modular architecture extension points (R4) supporting custom service integration for operational adaptability."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured through Eclipse Ditto's policy-based validation for sensor data (value ranges, units) and actuator command pre-execution checks. The Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, composed of timeliness (0.997), reliability (1.0), and availability (1.0), quantifies synchronization quality. Fault tolerance is provided by Eclipse Hono's at-least-once delivery and Ditto's persistent event storage. Bidirectional synchronization is validated via 1-second heartbeat mechanisms and ODTE measurements over 12-minute windows, confirming zero state divergence and integrity of communication channels."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono and Ditto are utilized for real-time bidirectional communication between physical assets and digital twins; network topology, bandwidth, latency requirements, reliability and redundancy mechanisms, and security protocols are not specified in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Modular architecture with standardized extension points supports scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, with runtime configuration management enabling adjustments without full redeployment; hosting platforms, computational and storage requirements, and deployment models are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Interoperability across heterogeneous systems via standardized BIM data schemas and bidirectional communication protocols, with modular architecture supporting standardized extension points for custom services and enterprise system integration."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono and Ditto ensure compliance with IoT communication standards for real-time data synchronization. BIM serves as the foundational AECO modeling standard, though explicit adherence to specific industry standards (e.g., IFC), certification requirements, or detailed compliance frameworks is not detailed in the documents."
]
